intro: Hypenia sclerophylla, also known as the 'Brazilian Lamiaceae', is a plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Hypenia sclerophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hypenia sclerophylla is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to utilize its health benefits, with some herbal preparations also incorporating it into topical creams and ointments for wound healing.
